steps.title: Identify corresponding angles in the similar triangles
steps.content: Triangle ABC ~ Triangle DEF with angle A corresponding to angle D. Since angles C and F are both right angles (90°), angles B and E are the two acute angles in their respective triangles.

steps.title: Use complementary angles in right triangles
steps.content:
In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary:
$$A + B = 90°$$
$$D + E = 90°$$
Since $A = D$ (corresponding angles), we have $E = 90° - D = 90° - A = B$.

steps.title: Find cos E
steps.content: Since $E = B$ and $\cos B = \frac{1}{22}$, we have $\cos E = \frac{1}{22}$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
